lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Interview

> Kim Brown introduces herself as the Oral History Committee chairperson for the Post Falls Historical Society and explains that the interview with Lewis M. Brown is part of the America 250 commemoration in Idaho. The project aims to engage the community and honor the state's contribution to America's history. The date of the interview is January 8th, 2025.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Interview
metadata.synopsis: Kim Brown introduces herself as the Oral History Committee chairperson for the Post Falls Historical Society and explains that the interview with Lewis M. Brown is part of the America 250 commemoration in Idaho. The project aims to engage the community and honor the state's contribution to America's history. The date of the interview is January 8th, 2025.

lines.line: # Family Immigration and Military Service

> The speaker's family immigrated to the United States in the late 1800s, with some members settling in Iowa and North Dakota before moving to Idaho. The speaker's relatives have served in the military, including the Civil War, World War I, World War II, and the Vietnam War, where the speaker himself was stationed in Alaska.

metadata.title: Family Immigration and Military Service
metadata.synopsis: The speaker's family immigrated to the United States in the late 1800s, with some members settling in Iowa and North Dakota before moving to Idaho. The speaker's relatives have served in the military, including the Civil War, World War I, World War II, and the Vietnam War, where the speaker himself was stationed in Alaska.

lines.line: # Parents' Roles at Luxor Lodge

> The speaker's father worked as a guide for the owners of the original Luxor Lodge, while his mother was a cook there. This was during a time when the highway had not yet been completed, making the lodge the end of the road. The speaker's father also worked for the Forest Service and was involved in trail construction and maintenance.

metadata.title: Parents' Roles at Luxor Lodge
metadata.synopsis: The speaker's father worked as a guide for the owners of the original Luxor Lodge, while his mother was a cook there. This was during a time when the highway had not yet been completed, making the lodge the end of the road. The speaker's father also worked for the Forest Service and was involved in trail construction and maintenance.

lines.line: # Family's Historical Connection to Local Geography

> The speaker's step-grandfather, Burt Wendover, has a geographic feature named after him, Wendover Ridge. The speaker also mentions Fred Schatz, a local trapper after whom Shot Creek was named, highlighting the family's deep ties to the local geography and history.

metadata.title: Family's Historical Connection to Local Geography
metadata.synopsis: The speaker's step-grandfather, Burt Wendover, has a geographic feature named after him, Wendover Ridge. The speaker also mentions Fred Schatz, a local trapper after whom Shot Creek was named, highlighting the family's deep ties to the local geography and history.

lines.line: # Personal Experiences in the Lochsa Area

> The speaker has visited the Lochsa area regularly since childhood, engaging in activities such as mining, prospecting, and backpacking. He has observed significant changes in the area, including a decline in the trail system and the impact of wilderness designation on access and vegetation.

metadata.title: Personal Experiences in the Lochsa Area
metadata.synopsis: The speaker has visited the Lochsa area regularly since childhood, engaging in activities such as mining, prospecting, and backpacking. He has observed significant changes in the area, including a decline in the trail system and the impact of wilderness designation on access and vegetation.

lines.line: # Military Service and Cultural Exposure

> The speaker's military service in Alaska during the Vietnam War exposed him to ethnic diversity and different climates. He recounts experiences with extreme cold, ice fog, and an earthquake. His military training was as a medic, and he worked in a hospital in Fairbanks.

metadata.title: Military Service and Cultural Exposure
metadata.synopsis: The speaker's military service in Alaska during the Vietnam War exposed him to ethnic diversity and different climates. He recounts experiences with extreme cold, ice fog, and an earthquake. His military training was as a medic, and he worked in a hospital in Fairbanks.

lines.line: # Post-Military Education and Employment

> After his military service, the speaker attended the University of Montana under the GI Bill and worked as an orderly in a hospital. He pursued a degree in wildlife management and began his career with the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Dillon, Montana, where he was involved in the Wild Horse and Burro program and environmental impact reporting.

metadata.title: Post-Military Education and Employment
metadata.synopsis: After his military service, the speaker attended the University of Montana under the GI Bill and worked as an orderly in a hospital. He pursued a degree in wildlife management and began his career with the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Dillon, Montana, where he was involved in the Wild Horse and Burro program and environmental impact reporting.

lines.line: # Career with the Bureau of Land Management

> The speaker's career with the BLM spanned 35 years, during which he worked on various projects, including timber sales, wildlife habitat mitigation, and the Bald Eagle monitoring program. He also participated in the Upper Columbia River project and witnessed the growth of the recreation program within the Coeur d'Alene district.

metadata.title: Career with the Bureau of Land Management
metadata.synopsis: The speaker's career with the BLM spanned 35 years, during which he worked on various projects, including timber sales, wildlife habitat mitigation, and the Bald Eagle monitoring program. He also participated in the Upper Columbia River project and witnessed the growth of the recreation program within the Coeur d'Alene district.

lines.line: # Reflections on Local Changes and Growth

> The speaker reflects on the significant population growth in the Post Falls area and the challenges it poses to the infrastructure. He notes the transformation of the landscape from grass fields to potential subdivisions and the changes in land use over the years.

metadata.title: Reflections on Local Changes and Growth
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the significant population growth in the Post Falls area and the challenges it poses to the infrastructure. He notes the transformation of the landscape from grass fields to potential subdivisions and the changes in land use over the years.

lines.line: # Thoughts on America 250 and Patriotic Participation

> The speaker expresses his willingness to participate in patriotic activities related to America 250, although he is not yet aware of specific events. He emphasizes the importance of recognizing historical achievements and preserving history for future generations.

metadata.title: Thoughts on America 250 and Patriotic Participation
metadata.synopsis: The speaker expresses his willingness to participate in patriotic activities related to America 250, although he is not yet aware of specific events. He emphasizes the importance of recognizing historical achievements and preserving history for future generations.
